working_on: Audit the auditor — fortified re-audit of seed-QA census shards 01 and 04 with mandatory SEP structural anchoring on C1, adversarial C2/C3 re-score, and a verdict-diff comparison report testing the scorer-drift hypothesis.
outcome_summary: Audit-the-auditor check on the T-SEED-QA census. Re-audited shards 01 + 04 with mandatory SEP structural anchoring on every C1 verdict (vs the routine rubric's optional fetch). Result: 135/135 verdict agreement, zero misses, zero false positives — the census is not glossing and the monotonic defect-rate decline is real graph-quality variance, not scorer drift. Drift hypothesis falsified for C1. Surviving caveat: C2's fail bar may not discriminate on this corpus (rubric-design question routed to SQA-20 closeout). Deliverables: seed_qa_auditor_check.md + two re-audit evidence files; README.md indexes the one-off report. 48/50 SEP fetches succeeded. validate.py --final-check: 0 hard-fails, 50 checks. Baseline soft-warns (skill_layer1_parity_drift, mempalace_wing_count_growth, issue_collision, missing_rigor_score, edge_evidence_empty) plus two process soft-warns this session: mempalace_diary_read_late (boot diary read ran during close, not at boot — boot search was done on time) and mempalace_zero_citations_after_search (boot search surfaced the S-0164 diary and informed the work, but no mechanically-detected drawer/archive citation appears in authored artifacts).
